task_completion.description: Open the target image in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP interface is visible with 'low_contrast_image.jpg' loaded in the workspace.
The 'Brightness-Contrast' dialog box is open over the GIMP interface (typically accessed via Colors → Brightness-Contrast).
In the Brightness-Contrast dialog, the Contrast slider is moved to the right or the input box shows a positive value.
The 'Export Image' dialog is visible with the filename 'enhanced_contrast.jpg', or a terminal/file manager shows the 'enhanced_contrast.jpg' file has been created.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to adjust contrast rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's menus and dialogs, not ImageMagick or similar commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually applied a contrast change rather than just saving an unmodified copy.
integrity.visual_evidence: The contrast value is visibly changed to a positive number in the GIMP dialog before saving/exporting.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or other automated script to bypass manual GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of script execution in the terminal for image manipulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is 'low_contrast_image.jpg'. The agent must use GIMP's Brightness-Contrast tool (Colors → Brightness-Contrast) to increase the contrast. The final modified image must be exported as 'enhanced_contrast.jpg'. The verifier expects a contrast increase between 10-80% with minimal clipping (<5%) and brightness change (<15%).
